claims.summary: LLM diagnostics should append durable JSONL without altering generated output.
claims.disposition: Verified existing best-effort append behavior and output-preserving failure path.

claims.summary: Persistence failures should expose bounded stage and IO-kind evidence without prose leakage.
claims.disposition: Verified existing bounded failure classification and no-content regression.

claims.summary: Diagnostic persistence should expose bounded serialized size and append latency.
claims.disposition: Implemented private metadata-only success and failure receipts with JSON bytes and monotonic microseconds.

claims.summary: High spectral entropy or felt viscosity should be treated as the cause of serialization failure.
claims.disposition: Rejected as unsupported causation; entropy and felt reports remain independent context.

claims.summary: Diagnostic append latency should be compared with naturally observed model or joint-trace stalls.
claims.disposition: Routed to bounded observation using the new metadata receipt; no stress or entropy induction.
